15	
16	The sun was low in the west by the time they saw the lake, its waters glimmering red and gold, bright as a sheet of beaten copper. When they glimpsed the turrets of the inn above some willows, Dunk donned his sweaty tunic once again and stopped to splash some water on his face. he washed off the dust of the road as best he could, and ran wet fingers through his thick mop of sun-streaked hair. There was nothing to be done for his size, or the scar that marked his cheek, but he wanted to make himself appear somewhat less the wild robber knight.
17	
18	The inn was bigger than he'd expected, a great gray sprawl of a place, timbered and turreted, half of it built on pilings out over the water. A road of rough-cut planks had been laid down over the muddy lakeshore to the ferry landing, but neither the ferry nor the ferrymen were in evidence. Across the road stood a stable with a thatched roof. A dry stone wall enclosed the yard, but the gate was open. Within, they found a well and a watering trough. "See to the animals," Dunk told Egg, "but see that they don't drink too much. I'll ask about some food."
19	
20	He found the innkeep sweeping off the steps. "Are you come for the ferry?" the woman asked him. "You're too late. The sun's going down, and Ned don't like to cross by night unless the moon is full. He'll be back first thing in the morning."
21	
22	"Do you know how much he asks?"
23	
24	"Three pennies for each of you, and ten for your horses."
25	
26	"We have two horses and a mule."
27	
28	"It's ten for mules as well."
29	
30	Dunk did the sums in his head, and came up with six-and-thirty, more than he had hoped to spend. "Last time I came this way, it was only two pennies, and six for horses."
31	
32	"Take that up with Ned, it's nought to me. If you're looking for a bed, I've none to offer. Lord Shawney and Lord Costayne brought their retinues. I'm full to bursting."
33	
34	"Is Lord Peake here as well?" He killed Ser. Arlan's squire. "He was with Lord Cockshaw and John the Fiddler."
35	
36	"Ned took them across on his last run." She looked Dunk up and down. "Were you part of their company?"
37	
38	"We net them on the road, is all." A good smell was drifting out the windows of the inn, one that made Dunk's mouth water. "We might like some of what you're roasting, if it's not too costly."
39	
40	"It's wild boar," the woman said, "well peppered, and served with onions, mushrooms, and mashed neeps."
41	
42	"We could do without the neeps. Some slices off the boar and a tankard of your good brown ale would do for us. How much would you ask for that? And maybe we could have a place on your stable floor to bed down for the night?"
43	
44	That was a mistake. "The stables are for horses. That's why we call them stables. You're big as a horse, I'll grant you, but I see only two legs." She swept her broom at him, to shoo him off. "I can't be expected to feed all the Seven Kingdoms. The boar is for my guests. So is my ale. I won't have lords saying that I run short of food or drink before they were surfeit. The lake is full of fish, and you'll find some other rogues camped down by the stumps. Hedge knights, if you believe them." Her tone made it quite clear that she did not. "Might be they'd have food to share. It's nought to me. Away with you now, I've work to do." The door closed with a solid thump behind her, before Dunk could even think to ask where he might find these stumps.
45	
46	He found Egg sitting on the horse trough, soaking his feet in the water and fanning his face with his big floppy hat. "Are they roasting pig, ser? I smell pork."
47	
48	"Wild boar," said Dunk in a glum tone, "but who wants boar when we have good salt beef?"
49	
50	Egg made a face. "Can I please eat my boots instead, ser? I'll make a new pair out of the salt beef. It's tougher."

He found his greathelm on the mule and slung it underhand at Egg. "Draw some water from the well and soak the beef." "Don't use the trough either, I don't care to taste your feet."
53	
54	"My feet could only improve the taste, ser," Egg said, wriggling his toes. But he did as he was bid.
55	
56	The hedge knights did not prove hard to find. Egg spied their fire flickering in the woods along the lakeshore, so they made for it, leading the animals behind them. The boy carried Dunk's helm beneath one arm, sloshing with each step he took. By then the sun was a red memory in the west. 57	
58	Amongst the weirwood stumps, they found two men squatting near a cook fire, passing a skin of wine from hand to hand. Their horses were cropping at the grass beyond the grove, and they had stacked their arms and armor in neat piles. A much younger man sat apart from the other two, his back against a chestnut tree. 59	
60	May we share your fire?"
61	
62	A stout man of middling years rose to greet them, garbed in tattered finery. Flamboyant ginger whiskers framed his face. "Well met, Ser Duncan. You are a large one ... and most welcome, certainly, as is your lad. Egg, was it? What sort of name is that, pray?"
63	
64	"A short one, ser." Egg knew better than to admit that Egg was short for Aegon. Not to men he did not know.
65	
66	"Indeed. What happened to your hair?"
67	
68	Rootworms, Dunk thought. Tell him it was rootworms, boy. That was the safest story, the tale they told most often ... though sometimes Egg took it in his head to play some childish game. "I shaved it off, ser. I mean to stay shaven until I earn my spurs."
69	
70	"A noble vow. I am Ser Kyle, the Cat of Misty Moor. Under yonder chestnut sits Ser Glendon, ah, Ball. And here you have the good Ser Maynard Plumm."
71	
72	Egg's ears pricked up at that name. "Plumm ... are you kin to Lord Viserys Plumm, ser?"
73	
74	"Distantly," confessed Ser Maynard, a tall, thin, stoop-shouldered man with long straight flaxen hair, "though I doubt that His Lordship would admit to it. One might say that he is of the sweet Plumms, whilst I am of the sour." Plumm's cloak was as purple as name, though frayed about the edges and badly dyed. A moonstone brooch big as a hen's egg fastened it at the shoulder.
75	
76	Elsewise he wore dun-colored roughspun and stained brown leather.
77	
78	"We have salt beef," said Dunk.
79	
80	"Ser Maynard has a bag of apples," said Kyle the Cat. "And I have pickled eggs and onions. Why, together we have the makings of a feast! Be seated, ser. We have a fine choice of stumps for your comfort. We will be here until midmorning, unless I miss my guess. There is only the one ferry, and it is not big enough to take us all. The lords and their tails must cross first."
81	
82	"Help me with the horses," Dunk told Egg. Together the two of them unsaddled Thunder, Rain, and Maester.
83	
84	Only when the animals had been fed and watered and hobbled for the night did Dunk accept the wineskin that Ser Maynard offered him. "Even sour wine is better than none," said Kyle the Cat. "We'll drink finer vintages at Whitewalls. Lord Butterwell is said to have the best wines north of the Arbor. He was once the King's Hand, as his father's father was before him, and he is said to be a pious man besides, and very rich."
85	
86	"His wealth is all from cows," said Maynard Plumm. "He ought to take a swollen udder for his arms. These Butterwells have milk running in their veins, and the Freys are no better. This will be a marriage of cattle thieves and toll collectors, one lot of coin clinkers joining with another.
87	
88	When the Black Dragon rose, this lord of cows sent one son to Daemon and one to Daeron, to make certain there was a Butterwell on the winning side. 89	
90	"As it should." Ser Glendon Ball gave his sword another stroke with the whetstone. "The Warrior hates cravens."
91	
92	The scorn in his voice made Dunk give the youth a closer look. Ser Glendon's clothes were of good cloth, but well-worn and ill-matched, with the look of hand-me-downs. Tufts of dark brown hair stuck out from beneath his iron halfhelm. The lad himself was short and chunky, with small close-set eyes, thick shoulders, and muscular arms. His eyebrows were shaggy as two caterpillars after a wet spring, his nose bulbous, his chin pugnacious. And he was young.
93	
94	Sixteen, might be. No more than eighteen. Dunk might have taken him for a squire if Ser Kyle had not named him with a ser. The lad had pimples on his cheeks in place of whiskers.
95	
96	"How long have you been a knight?" Dunk asked him.
97	
98	"Long enough. Half a year when the moon turns. I was knighted by Ser Morgan Dunstable of Tumbler's Falls, two dozen people saw it, but I have been training for knighthood since I was born. 99	
100	"The dragon's egg? Is that the champion's prize? Truly?" The last dragon had perished half a century ago. Ser Arlan had once seen a clutch of her eggs, though. 101	
102	"King Aegon presented the egg to his father's father after guesting for a night at his old castle," said Ser Maynard Plumm.
103	
104	"Was it a reward for some act of valor?" asked Dunk.
105	
106	Ser Kyle chuckled. "Some might call it that. Supposedly old Lord Butterwell had three young maiden daughters when His Grace came calling. By morning, all three had royal bastards in their little bellies. A hot night's work, that was."
107

110	"And who's to say we're not?" Ser Maynard quipped.
111

The sun was low in the west by the time they saw the lake, its waters glimmering red and gold, bright as a sheet of beaten copper. When they glimpsed the turrets of the inn above some willows, Dunk donned his sweaty tunic once again and stopped to splash some water on his face. he washed off the dust of the road as best he could, and ran wet fingers through his thick mop of sun-streaked hair. There was nothing to be done for his size, or the scar that marked his cheek, but he wanted to make himself appear somewhat less the wild robber knight.

The inn was bigger than he'd expected, a great gray sprawl of a place, timbered and turreted, half of it built on pilings out over the water. A road of rough-cut planks had been laid down over the muddy lakeshore to the ferry landing, but neither the ferry nor the ferrymen were in evidence. Across the road stood a stable with a thatched roof. A dry stone wall enclosed the yard, but the gate was open. Within, they found a well and a watering trough. He found the innkeep sweeping off the steps. "Are you come for the ferry?" the woman asked him. "You're too late. The sun's going down, and Ned don't like to cross by night unless the moon is full. He'll be back first thing in the morning."

"Do you know how much he asks?"

"Three pennies for each of you, and ten for your horses."

"We have two horses and a mule."

"It's ten for mules as well."

Dunk did the sums in his head, and came up with six-and-thirty, more than he had hoped to spend. "Last time I came this way, it was only two pennies, and six for horses."

"Take that up with Ned, it's nought to me. If you're looking for a bed, I've none to offer. Lord Shawney and Lord Costayne brought their retinues. I'm full to bursting."

"Is Lord Peake here as well?" He killed Ser. Arlan's squire. "He was with Lord Cockshaw and John the Fiddler."

"Ned took them across on his last run." She looked Dunk up and down. "Were you part of their company?"

"We net them on the road, is all." A good smell was drifting out the windows of the inn, one that made Dunk's mouth water. "We might like some of what you're roasting, if it's not too costly."

"It's wild boar," the woman said, "well peppered, and served with onions, mushrooms, and mashed neeps."

"We could do without the neeps. Some slices off the boar and a tankard of your good brown ale would do for us. How much would you ask for that? And maybe we could have a place on your stable floor to bed down for the night?"

That was a mistake. "The stables are for horses. That's why we call them stables. You're big as a horse, I'll grant you, but I see only two legs." She swept her broom at him, to shoo him off. "I can't be expected to feed all the Seven Kingdoms. The boar is for my guests. So is my ale. I won't have lords saying that I run short of food or drink before they were surfeit. "Might be they'd have food to share. It's nought to me. Away with you now, I've work to do." The door closed with a solid thump behind her, before Dunk could even think to ask where he might find these stumps.

He found Egg sitting on the horse trough, soaking his feet in the water and fanning his face with his big floppy hat. "Are they roasting pig, ser? I smell pork."

"Wild boar," said Dunk in a glum tone, "but who wants boar when we have good salt beef?"

"How can the truth be treason?" asked Kyle the Cat. "In King Daeron's day, a man did not have to fear to speak his mind, but now?" He made a rude noise. "Bloodraven put King Aerys on the Iron Throne, but for how long? Aerys is weak, and when he dies, it will be bloody war between Lord Rivers and Prince Maekar for the crown, the Hand against the heir."

"You have forgotten Prince Rhaegel, my friend," Ser Maynard objected, in a mild tone. "He comes next in line to Aerys, not Maekar, and his children after him."

"Rhaegel is feeble-minded. Why, I bear him no ill will, but the man is good as dead, and those twins of his as well, though whether they will die of Maekar's mace or Bloodraven's spells ..."

Seven save us, Dunk thought as Egg spoke up shrill and loud. "Prince Maekar is Prince Rhaegel's brother. He loves him well. He'd never do harm to him or his."

"Be quiet, boy," Dunk growled at him. "These knights want none of your opinions."

"I can talk if I want."

"No," said Dunk. "You can't." That mouth of yours will get you killed someday. And me as well, most like. "That salt beefs soaked long enough, I think. A strip for all our friends, and be quick about it."

Egg flushed, and for half a heartbeat, Dunk feared the boy might talk back. Instead he settled for a sullen look, seething as only a boy of eleven years can seethe. "Aye, ser," he said, fishing in the bottom of Dunk's helm. His shaven head shone redly in the firelight as he passed out the salt beef.

Dunk took his piece and worried at it. The soak had turned the meat from wood to leather, but that was all. He sucked on one corner, tasting the salt and trying not to think about the roast boar at the inn, crackling on its spit and dripping fat.

As dusk deepened, flies and stinging midges came swarming off the lake. The flies preferred to plague their horses, but the midges had a taste for man flesh. The only way to keep from being bitten was to sit close to the fire, breathing smoke. Cook or be devoured, Dunk thought glumly, now there's a beggar's choice. He scratched at his arms and edged closer to the fire.

The wineskin soon came round again. The wine was sour and strong. Dunk drank deep, and passed along the skin, whilst the Cat of Misty Moor began to talk of how he had saved the life of the Lord of Bitterbridge during the Backfire Rebellion. "When Lord Armond's banner-bearer fell, I leapt down from my horse with traitors all around us. . ."

"Ser," said Glendon Ball. "Who were these traitors?"

"The Blackfyre men, I meant."

Firelight glimmered off the steel in Ser Glendon's hand. The pock-marks on his face flamed as red as open sores, and his every sinew was wound as tight as a crossbow. "My father fought for the Black Dragon."

This again. Dunk snorted. Red or Black? was not a thing you asked a man. It always made for trouble. "I am sure Ser Kyle meant no insult to your father."

"None," Ser Kyle agreed. "It's an old tale, the Red Dragon and the Black. No sense for us to fight about it now, lad. We are all brothers of the hedges here."

Ser Glendon seemed to weigh the Cat's words, to see if he was being mocked. "Daemon Blackfyre was no traitor. The old king gave him the sword. He saw the worthiness in Daemon, even though he was born bastard. Why else would he put Blackfyre into his hand in place of Daeron's? He meant for him to have the kingdom too. Daemon was the better man."

A hush fell. Dunk could hear the soft crackle of the fire. He could feel midges crawling on the back of his neck. He slapped at them, watching Egg, willing him to be still. "I was just a boy when they fought the Red-grass Field," he said, when it seemed that no one else would speak,

"but I squired for a knight who fought with the Red Dragon, and later served another who fought for the Black. There were brave men on both sides."

"Brave men," echoed Kyle the Cat, a bit feebly.

"Heroes." Glendon Ball turned his shield about, so all of them could see the sigil painted there, a fireball blazing red and yellow across a night-black field. "I come from hero's blood."

"You're Fireball's son," Egg said.

That was the first time they saw Ser Glendon smile.

Ser Kyle the Cat studied the boy closely. "How can that be? How old are you? Quentyn Ball died. . ."

". . .before I was born," Ser Glendon finished, "but in me, he lives again." He slammed his sword back into its scabbard. "I'll show you all at Whitewalls, when I claim the dragon's egg."

## Events & Actions
1. **Arrival at the lakeshore inn** — At sundown Dunk and Egg see the lake and the inn's turrets; Dunk dons his sweaty tunic and washes the road dust from his face to look "less the wild robber knight."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Location: The lakeshore / inn approach
2. **Egg sees to the animals** — Dunk tells Egg to water the animals but not let them drink too much; Egg tends them at the yard's well and trough.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: The horses and mule
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The inn's stable yard
3. **Dunk bargains with the innkeep** — Dunk learns the day's last ferry (run by Ned) is gone until morning, that the fares have risen (three pennies each, ten per horse and mule — "six-and-thirty"), and asks to buy boar and a place to sleep.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Innkeep
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Refused
4. **The innkeep refuses and shoos Dunk off** — She has no bed (full of lords' retinues) and reserves the boar and ale for her guests, sweeping her broom at Dunk and directing him to the hedge knights "down by the stumps."
   - Agent: Innkeep
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Instrument: Her broom
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Dunk sent away; door shut "with a solid thump"
5. **Egg soaks the salt beef** — Dunk finds Egg soaking his feet in the trough, slings him the greathelm, and orders him to draw well water and soak the salt beef (not in the foot-trough).
   - Agent: Egg
   - Instrument: Dunk's greathelm; the well
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The stable yard
6. **Finding the hedge knights' camp** — Egg spies the fire in the woods along the lakeshore; they lead the animals to it and reach the old weirwood grove.
   - Agent: Egg (spots the fire); Dunk and Egg (travel)
   - Location: Woods along the lakeshore / weirwood grove
7. **Introductions at the fire** — Dunk hails the armed men openly, names himself Ser Duncan the Tall and "the lad is Egg," and asks to share their fire; Ser Kyle greets them and names Ser Glendon Ball and Ser Maynard Plumm.
   - Agent: Dunk; Ser Kyle
   - Patient: Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon (met)
   - Location: The weirwood grove
   - Outcome: Welcomed to the fire
8. **Egg conceals his identity** — Asked his odd name and his baldness, Egg withholds that "Egg" is short for Aegon and gives the shaven-for-spurs story rather than the usual "rootworms" tale.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Ser Kyle (and the camp)
   - Outcome: Identity kept hidden
9. **Unsaddling and provisioning the animals** — Dunk and Egg unsaddle, feed, water, and hobble Thunder, Rain, and Maester for the night.
   - Agent: Dunk and Egg
   - Patient: Thunder, Rain, Maester
   - Location: The grove
10. **The shared meal and fireside talk** — The men pool salt beef, apples, pickled eggs, onions, and wine and discuss the Whitewalls wedding, the dragon's-egg prize, the ironmen's raids, and the realm's rulers.
    - Agent: Dunk, Egg, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon
    - Location: The grove camp
11. **Egg speaks out about Prince Maekar; Dunk silences him** — When Kyle says Rhaegel's twins may die "of Maekar's mace," Egg protests loudly that Maekar loves his brother; Dunk growls him quiet and sets him to passing out the beef.
    - Agent: Egg (speaks); Dunk (silences)
    - Patient: Egg
    - Instigator: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Egg falls sullenly silent
12. **Ser Kyle names Blackfyre men "traitors"; Glendon takes offense** — Kyle calls the Blackfyre men traitors while telling of the Backfire Rebellion; Glendon, blade in hand and pock-marks reddening, declares his father fought for the Black Dragon and that Daemon was no traitor.
    - Agent: Ser Kyle (gives offense); Ser Glendon (reacts)
    - Patient: Ser Glendon
    - Instrument: Ser Glendon's drawn sword
    - Witness: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Tension; Dunk defuses it
13. **Glendon reveals himself as Fireball's son** — Glendon turns his shield to show the fireball sigil, says "I come from hero's blood"; Egg names him "Fireball's son," and Glendon confirms he is Quentyn Ball's son, vowing to claim the dragon's egg at Whitewalls.
    - Agent: Ser Glendon (reveals); Egg (names him)
    - Instrument: Glendon's fireball shield
    - Witness: Dunk, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Glendon's parentage revealed; he smiles for the first time

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Weary and travel-worn, hungry (his mouth waters at the boar; he sucks salt beef and tries "not to think about the roast boar"); glum; wary of armed strangers; amused by Egg ("trying not to smile"); increasingly anxious as the fireside talk turns to treason and to Egg's family.
- **Primary preoccupation:** Getting food and a cheap place to sleep, and keeping Egg quiet and his identity hidden; concern over the ferry cost ("more than he had hoped to spend").
- **Key decisions made:** To camp with the hedge knights; to soak the salt beef; to keep his and Egg's business vague ("We net them on the road, is all"); to hold to the northward journey to Winterfell rather than divert to Whitewalls; to defuse the Red/Black quarrel; to silence Egg.
- **Self-deception flags:** "Perhaps he's finally learned to hold that tongue of his" — immediately disproved when Egg speaks out about Prince Maekar; "Red or Black? was not a thing you asked a man" — reflecting on trouble he then has to smooth over.

# TMK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TMK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Lakeshore inn and the weirwood-grove camp beside the lake (the ferry crossing)
- **location_secondary:** The inn's stable and yard; the ferry landing; (mentioned) Whitewalls, Winterfell, Summerhall, Tyrosh, Stoney Sept, Bitterbridge, Tumbler's Falls, Redgrass Field, the Arbor, Dorne, Oldtown
- **approximate_timeline:** Evening into night; unit part 2 of 9. Dunk and Egg reach the lakeshore inn at sundown, learn they have missed the day's last ferry, are refused food and lodging by the innkeep, and make camp for the night among the hedge knights in the old weirwood grove while awaiting the morning ferry.
- **time_markers:** "The sun was low in the west"; "The sun's going down"; "He'll be back first thing in the morning"; "the sun was a red memory in the west"; "We will be here until midmorning"; "As dusk deepened"; "The last dragon had perished half a century ago"; Glendon knighted "Half a year when the moon turns"; Butterwell "was once the King's Hand, as his father's father was before him"; "When the Black Dragon rose"; "Both perished on the Redgrass Field"; "his youngest died in the spring"; "Quentyn Ball died ... before I was born"; "In King Daeron's day"; "the Backfire Rebellion"; Glendon "Sixteen, might be. No more than eighteen"; Egg "a boy of eleven years"
- **chapter_summary:** Dunk and Egg reach a large lakeshore inn at sundown but have missed the day's last ferry, run by a man named Ned, and the innkeep — full up with Lord Shawney's and Lord Costayne's retinues — refuses to sell them the roast boar or give them a bed. She directs them to hedge knights camped by the stumps down the shore. In an old weirwood grove Dunk and Egg join Ser Kyle the Cat of Misty Moor, Ser Maynard Plumm, and the young Ser Glendon Ball, sharing salt beef, apples, pickled eggs, onions and sour wine. Over the fire the men discuss the coming wedding of Lord Butterwell to a Frey at Whitewalls, where a dragon's egg is the champion's prize, and trade talk of the realm's troubles — the ironmen's raids, King Aerys, the princes, and the Hand, Bloodraven. Glendon reveals himself as the son of Fireball (Quentyn Ball), who fought for the Black Dragon, and vows to claim the dragon's egg at Whitewalls.
- **unit_part:** 2 of 9

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Warm; midges and flies swarm off the lake at dusk
- **Season indicators:** Summer implied by warmth and biting insects; "his youngest died in the spring" (recalled)
- **Time of day:** Late afternoon into full night (sundown, then dusk, then dark)
- **Lighting:** The setting sun turns the lake "red and gold, bright as a sheet of beaten copper"; "the sun was a red memory in the west"; firelight in the grove; Egg's shaven head "shone redly in the firelight"; steel glimmering in the firelight
- **Sounds:** Good smell aside — the innkeep's broom sweeping; the door closing "with a solid thump"; the soft crackle of the cook fire; the whetstone on Glendon's sword
- **Smells:** A good smell of roasting wild boar drifting from the inn windows ("made Dunk's mouth water"); the roast "crackling on its spit and dripping fat"; sour wine; salt beef
- **Notable sensory details:** The lake glimmering red and gold like beaten copper; flies and stinging midges rising off the lake at dusk (flies plague the horses, midges bite men); the only relief is sitting in the fire's smoke ("Cook or be devoured"); midges crawling on the back of Dunk's neck; the ring of white weirwood stumps and bone-pale roots in the grove

## Events & Actions
1. **Arrival at the lakeshore inn** — At sundown Dunk and Egg see the lake and the inn's turrets; Dunk dons his sweaty tunic and washes the road dust from his face to look "less the wild robber knight."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Location: The lakeshore / inn approach
2. **Egg sees to the animals** — Dunk tells Egg to water the animals but not let them drink too much; Egg tends them at the yard's well and trough.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: The horses and mule
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The inn's stable yard
3. **Dunk bargains with the innkeep** — Dunk learns the day's last ferry (run by Ned) is gone until morning, that the fares have risen (three pennies each, ten per horse and mule — "six-and-thirty"), and asks to buy boar and a place to sleep.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Innkeep
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Refused
4. **The innkeep refuses and shoos Dunk off** — She has no bed (full of lords' retinues) and reserves the boar and ale for her guests, sweeping her broom at Dunk and directing him to the hedge knights "down by the stumps."
   - Agent: Innkeep
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Instrument: Her broom
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Dunk sent away; door shut "with a solid thump"
5. **Egg soaks the salt beef** — Dunk finds Egg soaking his feet in the trough, slings him the greathelm, and orders him to draw well water and soak the salt beef (not in the foot-trough).
   - Agent: Egg
   - Instrument: Dunk's greathelm; the well
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The stable yard
6. **Finding the hedge knights' camp** — Egg spies the fire in the woods along the lakeshore; they lead the animals to it and reach the old weirwood grove.
   - Agent: Egg (spots the fire); Dunk and Egg (travel)
   - Location: Woods along the lakeshore / weirwood grove
7. **Introductions at the fire** — Dunk hails the armed men openly, names himself Ser Duncan the Tall and "the lad is Egg," and asks to share their fire; Ser Kyle greets them and names Ser Glendon Ball and Ser Maynard Plumm.
   - Agent: Dunk; Ser Kyle
   - Patient: Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon (met)
   - Location: The weirwood grove
   - Outcome: Welcomed to the fire
8. **Egg conceals his identity** — Asked his odd name and his baldness, Egg withholds that "Egg" is short for Aegon and gives the shaven-for-spurs story rather than the usual "rootworms" tale.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Ser Kyle (and the camp)
   - Outcome: Identity kept hidden
9. **Unsaddling and provisioning the animals** — Dunk and Egg unsaddle, feed, water, and hobble Thunder, Rain, and Maester for the night.
   - Agent: Dunk and Egg
   - Patient: Thunder, Rain, Maester
   - Location: The grove
10. **The shared meal and fireside talk** — The men pool salt beef, apples, pickled eggs, onions, and wine and discuss the Whitewalls wedding, the dragon's-egg prize, the ironmen's raids, and the realm's rulers.
    - Agent: Dunk, Egg, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon
    - Location: The grove camp
11. **Egg speaks out about Prince Maekar; Dunk silences him** — When Kyle says Rhaegel's twins may die "of Maekar's mace," Egg protests loudly that Maekar loves his brother; Dunk growls him quiet and sets him to passing out the beef.
    - Agent: Egg (speaks); Dunk (silences)
    - Patient: Egg
    - Instigator: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Egg falls sullenly silent
12. **Ser Kyle names Blackfyre men "traitors"; Glendon takes offense** — Kyle calls the Blackfyre men traitors while telling of the Backfire Rebellion; Glendon, blade in hand and pock-marks reddening, declares his father fought for the Black Dragon and that Daemon was no traitor.
    - Agent: Ser Kyle (gives offense); Ser Glendon (reacts)
    - Patient: Ser Glendon
    - Instrument: Ser Glendon's drawn sword
    - Witness: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Tension; Dunk defuses it
13. **Glendon reveals himself as Fireball's son** — Glendon turns his shield to show the fireball sigil, says "I come from hero's blood"; Egg names him "Fireball's son," and Glendon confirms he is Quentyn Ball's son, vowing to claim the dragon's egg at Whitewalls.
    - Agent: Ser Glendon (reveals); Egg (names him)
    - Instrument: Glendon's fireball shield
    - Witness: Dunk, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Glendon's parentage revealed; he smiles for the first time

# TMK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TMK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Lakeshore inn and the weirwood-grove camp beside the lake (the ferry crossing)
- **location_secondary:** The inn's stable and yard; the ferry landing; (mentioned) Whitewalls, Winterfell, Summerhall, Tyrosh, Stoney Sept, Bitterbridge, Tumbler's Falls, Redgrass Field, the Arbor, Dorne, Oldtown
- **approximate_timeline:** Evening into night; unit part 2 of 9. Dunk and Egg reach the lakeshore inn at sundown, learn they have missed the day's last ferry, are refused food and lodging by the innkeep, and make camp for the night among the hedge knights in the old weirwood grove while awaiting the morning ferry.
- **time_markers:** "The sun was low in the west"; "The sun's going down"; "He'll be back first thing in the morning"; "the sun was a red memory in the west"; "We will be here until midmorning"; "As dusk deepened"; "The last dragon had perished half a century ago"; Glendon knighted "Half a year when the moon turns"; Butterwell "was once the King's Hand, as his father's father was before him"; "When the Black Dragon rose"; "Both perished on the Redgrass Field"; "his youngest died in the spring"; "Quentyn Ball died ... before I was born"; "In King Daeron's day"; "the Backfire Rebellion"; Glendon "Sixteen, might be. No more than eighteen"; Egg "a boy of eleven years"
- **chapter_summary:** Dunk and Egg reach a large lakeshore inn at sundown but have missed the day's last ferry, run by a man named Ned, and the innkeep — full up with Lord Shawney's and Lord Costayne's retinues — refuses to sell them the roast boar or give them a bed. She directs them to hedge knights camped by the stumps down the shore. In an old weirwood grove Dunk and Egg join Ser Kyle the Cat of Misty Moor, Ser Maynard Plumm, and the young Ser Glendon Ball, sharing salt beef, apples, pickled eggs, onions and sour wine. Over the fire the men discuss the coming wedding of Lord Butterwell to a Frey at Whitewalls, where a dragon's egg is the champion's prize, and trade talk of the realm's troubles — the ironmen's raids, King Aerys, the princes, and the Hand, Bloodraven. Glendon reveals himself as the son of Fireball (Quentyn Ball), who fought for the Black Dragon, and vows to claim the dragon's egg at Whitewalls.
- **unit_part:** 2 of 9

- **Sounds:** Good smell aside — the innkeep's broom sweeping; the door closing "with a solid thump"; the soft crackle of the cook fire; the whetstone on Glendon's sword
- **Smells:** A good smell of roasting wild boar drifting from the inn windows ("made Dunk's mouth water"); the roast "crackling on its spit and dripping fat"; sour wine; salt beef
- **Notable sensory details:** The lake glimmering red and gold like beaten copper; flies and stinging midges rising off the lake at dusk (flies plague the horses, midges bite men); the only relief is sitting in the fire's smoke ("Cook or be devoured"); midges crawling on the back of Dunk's neck; the ring of white weirwood stumps and bone-pale roots in the grove

## Events & Actions
1. **Arrival at the lakeshore inn** — At sundown Dunk and Egg see the lake and the inn's turrets; Dunk dons his sweaty tunic and washes the road dust from his face to look "less the wild robber knight."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Location: The lakeshore / inn approach
2. **Egg sees to the animals** — Dunk tells Egg to water the animals but not let them drink too much; Egg tends them at the yard's well and trough.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: The horses and mule
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The inn's stable yard
3. **Dunk bargains with the innkeep** — Dunk learns the day's last ferry (run by Ned) is gone until morning, that the fares have risen (three pennies each, ten per horse and mule — "six-and-thirty"), and asks to buy boar and a place to sleep.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Innkeep
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Refused
4. **The innkeep refuses and shoos Dunk off** — She has no bed (full of lords' retinues) and reserves the boar and ale for her guests, sweeping her broom at Dunk and directing him to the hedge knights "down by the stumps."
   - Agent: Innkeep
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Instrument: Her broom
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Dunk sent away; door shut "with a solid thump"
5. **Egg soaks the salt beef** — Dunk finds Egg soaking his feet in the trough, slings him the greathelm, and orders him to draw well water and soak the salt beef (not in the foot-trough).
   - Agent: Egg
   - Instrument: Dunk's greathelm; the well
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The stable yard
6. **Finding the hedge knights' camp** — Egg spies the fire in the woods along the lakeshore; they lead the animals to it and reach the old weirwood grove.
   - Agent: Egg (spots the fire); Dunk and Egg (travel)
   - Location: Woods along the lakeshore / weirwood grove
7. **Introductions at the fire** — Dunk hails the armed men openly, names himself Ser Duncan the Tall and "the lad is Egg," and asks to share their fire; Ser Kyle greets them and names Ser Glendon Ball and Ser Maynard Plumm.
   - Agent: Dunk; Ser Kyle
   - Patient: Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon (met)
   - Location: The weirwood grove
   - Outcome: Welcomed to the fire
8. **Egg conceals his identity** — Asked his odd name and his baldness, Egg withholds that "Egg" is short for Aegon and gives the shaven-for-spurs story rather than the usual "rootworms" tale.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Ser Kyle (and the camp)
   - Outcome: Identity kept hidden
9. **Unsaddling and provisioning the animals** — Dunk and Egg unsaddle, feed, water, and hobble Thunder, Rain, and Maester for the night.
   - Agent: Dunk and Egg
   - Patient: Thunder, Rain, Maester
   - Location: The grove
10. **The shared meal and fireside talk** — The men pool salt beef, apples, pickled eggs, onions, and wine and discuss the Whitewalls wedding, the dragon's-egg prize, the ironmen's raids, and the realm's rulers.
    - Agent: Dunk, Egg, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon
    - Location: The grove camp
11. **Egg speaks out about Prince Maekar; Dunk silences him** — When Kyle says Rhaegel's twins may die "of Maekar's mace," Egg protests loudly that Maekar loves his brother; Dunk growls him quiet and sets him to passing out the beef.
    - Agent: Egg (speaks); Dunk (silences)
    - Patient: Egg
    - Instigator: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Egg falls sullenly silent
12. **Ser Kyle names Blackfyre men "traitors"; Glendon takes offense** — Kyle calls the Blackfyre men traitors while telling of the Backfire Rebellion; Glendon, blade in hand and pock-marks reddening, declares his father fought for the Black Dragon and that Daemon was no traitor.
    - Agent: Ser Kyle (gives offense); Ser Glendon (reacts)
    - Patient: Ser Glendon
    - Instrument: Ser Glendon's drawn sword
    - Witness: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Tension; Dunk defuses it
13. **Glendon reveals himself as Fireball's son** — Glendon turns his shield to show the fireball sigil, says "I come from hero's blood"; Egg names him "Fireball's son," and Glendon confirms he is Quentyn Ball's son, vowing to claim the dragon's egg at Whitewalls.
    - Agent: Ser Glendon (reveals); Egg (names him)
    - Instrument: Glendon's fireball shield
    - Witness: Dunk, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Glendon's parentage revealed; he smiles for the first time

## POV Character's Internal State
- **Emotional state:** Weary and travel-worn, hungry (his mouth waters at the boar; he sucks salt beef and tries "not to think about the roast boar"); glum; wary of armed strangers; amused by Egg ("trying not to smile"); increasingly anxious as the fireside talk turns to treason and to Egg's family.
- **Primary preoccupation:** Getting food and a cheap place to sleep, and keeping Egg quiet and his identity hidden; concern over the ferry cost ("more than he had hoped to spend").
- **Key decisions made:** To camp with the hedge knights; to soak the salt beef; to keep his and Egg's business vague ("We net them on the road, is all"); to hold to the northward journey to Winterfell rather than divert to Whitewalls; to defuse the Red/Black quarrel; to silence Egg.

# TMK — Dunk 1

## Chapter Metadata
- **book:** TMK
- **chapter_number:** 1
- **pov_character:** Dunk
- **pov_chapter_number:** Dunk I
- **first_available:** pre-agot
- **location_primary:** Lakeshore inn and the weirwood-grove camp beside the lake (the ferry crossing)
- **location_secondary:** The inn's stable and yard; the ferry landing; (mentioned) Whitewalls, Winterfell, Summerhall, Tyrosh, Stoney Sept, Bitterbridge, Tumbler's Falls, Redgrass Field, the Arbor, Dorne, Oldtown
- **approximate_timeline:** Evening into night; unit part 2 of 9. Dunk and Egg reach the lakeshore inn at sundown, learn they have missed the day's last ferry, are refused food and lodging by the innkeep, and make camp for the night among the hedge knights in the old weirwood grove while awaiting the morning ferry.
- **time_markers:** "The sun was low in the west"; "The sun's going down"; "He'll be back first thing in the morning"; "the sun was a red memory in the west"; "We will be here until midmorning"; "As dusk deepened"; "The last dragon had perished half a century ago"; Glendon knighted "Half a year when the moon turns"; Butterwell "was once the King's Hand, as his father's father was before him"; "When the Black Dragon rose"; "Both perished on the Redgrass Field"; "his youngest died in the spring"; "Quentyn Ball died ... before I was born"; "In King Daeron's day"; "the Backfire Rebellion"; Glendon "Sixteen, might be. No more than eighteen"; Egg "a boy of eleven years"
- **chapter_summary:** Dunk and Egg reach a large lakeshore inn at sundown but have missed the day's last ferry, run by a man named Ned, and the innkeep — full up with Lord Shawney's and Lord Costayne's retinues — refuses to sell them the roast boar or give them a bed. She directs them to hedge knights camped by the stumps down the shore. In an old weirwood grove Dunk and Egg join Ser Kyle the Cat of Misty Moor, Ser Maynard Plumm, and the young Ser Glendon Ball, sharing salt beef, apples, pickled eggs, onions and sour wine. Over the fire the men discuss the coming wedding of Lord Butterwell to a Frey at Whitewalls, where a dragon's egg is the champion's prize, and trade talk of the realm's troubles — the ironmen's raids, King Aerys, the princes, and the Hand, Bloodraven. Glendon reveals himself as the son of Fireball (Quentyn Ball), who fought for the Black Dragon, and vows to claim the dragon's egg at Whitewalls.
- **unit_part:** 2 of 9

## Physical Environment
- **Weather:** Warm; midges and flies swarm off the lake at dusk
- **Season indicators:** Summer implied by warmth and biting insects; "his youngest died in the spring" (recalled)
- **Time of day:** Late afternoon into full night (sundown, then dusk, then dark)
- **Lighting:** The setting sun turns the lake "red and gold, bright as a sheet of beaten copper"; "the sun was a red memory in the west"; firelight in the grove; Egg's shaven head "shone redly in the firelight"; steel glimmering in the firelight
- **Sounds:** Good smell aside — the innkeep's broom sweeping; the door closing "with a solid thump"; the soft crackle of the cook fire; the whetstone on Glendon's sword
- **Smells:** A good smell of roasting wild boar drifting from the inn windows ("made Dunk's mouth water"); the roast "crackling on its spit and dripping fat"; sour wine; salt beef
- **Notable sensory details:** The lake glimmering red and gold like beaten copper; flies and stinging midges rising off the lake at dusk (flies plague the horses, midges bite men); the only relief is sitting in the fire's smoke ("Cook or be devoured"); midges crawling on the back of Dunk's neck; the ring of white weirwood stumps and bone-pale roots in the grove

## Events & Actions
1. **Arrival at the lakeshore inn** — At sundown Dunk and Egg see the lake and the inn's turrets; Dunk dons his sweaty tunic and washes the road dust from his face to look "less the wild robber knight."
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Location: The lakeshore / inn approach
2. **Egg sees to the animals** — Dunk tells Egg to water the animals but not let them drink too much; Egg tends them at the yard's well and trough.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: The horses and mule
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The inn's stable yard
3. **Dunk bargains with the innkeep** — Dunk learns the day's last ferry (run by Ned) is gone until morning, that the fares have risen (three pennies each, ten per horse and mule — "six-and-thirty"), and asks to buy boar and a place to sleep.
   - Agent: Dunk
   - Patient: Innkeep
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Refused
4. **The innkeep refuses and shoos Dunk off** — She has no bed (full of lords' retinues) and reserves the boar and ale for her guests, sweeping her broom at Dunk and directing him to the hedge knights "down by the stumps."
   - Agent: Innkeep
   - Patient: Dunk
   - Instrument: Her broom
   - Location: The inn steps
   - Outcome: Dunk sent away; door shut "with a solid thump"
5. **Egg soaks the salt beef** — Dunk finds Egg soaking his feet in the trough, slings him the greathelm, and orders him to draw well water and soak the salt beef (not in the foot-trough).
   - Agent: Egg
   - Instrument: Dunk's greathelm; the well
   - Instigator: Dunk
   - Location: The stable yard
6. **Finding the hedge knights' camp** — Egg spies the fire in the woods along the lakeshore; they lead the animals to it and reach the old weirwood grove.
   - Agent: Egg (spots the fire); Dunk and Egg (travel)
   - Location: Woods along the lakeshore / weirwood grove
7. **Introductions at the fire** — Dunk hails the armed men openly, names himself Ser Duncan the Tall and "the lad is Egg," and asks to share their fire; Ser Kyle greets them and names Ser Glendon Ball and Ser Maynard Plumm.
   - Agent: Dunk; Ser Kyle
   - Patient: Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon (met)
   - Location: The weirwood grove
   - Outcome: Welcomed to the fire
8. **Egg conceals his identity** — Asked his odd name and his baldness, Egg withholds that "Egg" is short for Aegon and gives the shaven-for-spurs story rather than the usual "rootworms" tale.
   - Agent: Egg
   - Patient: Ser Kyle (and the camp)
   - Outcome: Identity kept hidden
9. **Unsaddling and provisioning the animals** — Dunk and Egg unsaddle, feed, water, and hobble Thunder, Rain, and Maester for the night.
   - Agent: Dunk and Egg
   - Patient: Thunder, Rain, Maester
   - Location: The grove
10. **The shared meal and fireside talk** — The men pool salt beef, apples, pickled eggs, onions, and wine and discuss the Whitewalls wedding, the dragon's-egg prize, the ironmen's raids, and the realm's rulers.
    - Agent: Dunk, Egg, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard, Ser Glendon
    - Location: The grove camp
11. **Egg speaks out about Prince Maekar; Dunk silences him** — When Kyle says Rhaegel's twins may die "of Maekar's mace," Egg protests loudly that Maekar loves his brother; Dunk growls him quiet and sets him to passing out the beef.
    - Agent: Egg (speaks); Dunk (silences)
    - Patient: Egg
    - Instigator: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Egg falls sullenly silent
12. **Ser Kyle names Blackfyre men "traitors"; Glendon takes offense** — Kyle calls the Blackfyre men traitors while telling of the Backfire Rebellion; Glendon, blade in hand and pock-marks reddening, declares his father fought for the Black Dragon and that Daemon was no traitor.
    - Agent: Ser Kyle (gives offense); Ser Glendon (reacts)
    - Patient: Ser Glendon
    - Instrument: Ser Glendon's drawn sword
    - Witness: Dunk
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Tension; Dunk defuses it
13. **Glendon reveals himself as Fireball's son** — Glendon turns his shield to show the fireball sigil, says "I come from hero's blood"; Egg names him "Fireball's son," and Glendon confirms he is Quentyn Ball's son, vowing to claim the dragon's egg at Whitewalls.
    - Agent: Ser Glendon (reveals); Egg (names him)
    - Instrument: Glendon's fireball shield
    - Witness: Dunk, Ser Kyle, Ser Maynard
    - Location: The grove camp
    - Outcome: Glendon's parentage revealed; he smiles for the first time
